observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

We recommended that management:

Enjoin the Municipal Assessor to fast track the preparation of the updated Assessment Roll and provide a copy to the Municipal Treasurer as basis for updating the RPTARs/RPTICs; 

Thereafter direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

Update the RPTARs/RPTICs and prepare a duly certified list of taxpayers with corresponding amount of tax due and collectible for the year which shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in the set-up of RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year; and

Prepare a complete list of Delinquent Taxpayers and post in appropriate public places as required under Section 254 of R.A. 7160.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Assessor overlooked to furnish copy of the Assessment Roll to the Municipal Treasurer as of December 31, 2024. 





Non-receipt by the Municipal Treasurer of Assessment Roll as of December 31, 2024 from the Municipal Assessor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management take immediate steps to dispose all unserviceable properties taking into consideration the procedures prescribed in the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties per COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2024-1 to avoid further deterioration and reduction in values of the subject properties.
observations.reason: Lack of knowledge on disposal of unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management direct the MDRRMO to incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the detailed planned projects/program/activities chargeable to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years and those funds under the Trust Liability – DRRMF.
observations.reason: Not prioritized by the LDRRMO.
No available records on detailed description and cost segregation for prior years Local Road Network

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Loal Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to file an application for Land Bank’s weAccess online facility for real-time viewing of account statements and improve lead time in the preparation of BRS and recording of reconciling items in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Assessor to provide copies of tax declarations for land properties owned by the Municipality to the Municipal Accountant, as basis for the latter to establish a subsidiary list and as basis to elect the revaluation model in accounting for land as prescribed under IPSAS 17 using latest available Fair Market Value; and
Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to provide appropriate disclosures in the financial statements in accordance with the disclosure requirements of IPSAS 17.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Create an inventory committee composed of adequate number of members, with members from the Accounting Unit and GSO, and enjoin them to:
Conduct physical inventory count at least once a year and submit to the Audit Team the corresponding Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and Report on Physical Count of Local Road Networks for local roads; and
Expedite the One-Time Cleansing of the PPE account in accordance with the procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006, to ensure that the PPE balances are fairly presented in the financial statements.
Require the GSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to reconcile PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards regularly.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Maintain separate Subsidiary Ledgers for each economic enterprise for the accounts Due to GSIS, Due to Philhealth and Due to Pag-IBIG and observe correct accounting entries in the recording of transactions; and
Trace, insofar as practicable, the discrepancies noted in Due to GSIS, Due to Philhealth and Due to Pag-IBIG accounts and draw necessary correcting entries to reflect the adjusted balance.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to provide adequate disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements to achieve fair presentation of the financial statements.

In light of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure timely remittance of Pag-IBIG contributions and loan payments of JO personnel by assigning a personnel to oversee the monthly remittance to Pag-IBIG Pagadian Branch within the prescribed deadline;
Instruct the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to strengthen coordination with Accounting Unit and GSIS to ensure that salary adjustments and other updates are reflected in the GSIS billing immediately; and
Require the Accounting Unit to regularly compare its records with GSIS billing statements to promptly identify and address any discrepancies.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Human Resource Management Officer to develop a standardized Job Order contract that clearly outlines the specific duties and responsibilities, and work schedules including provisions for overtime to ensure clarity and enforceability of engagements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the MDRRMO and LDRRM Council to ensure that charges to the LDRRM Fund are strictly in compliance with Section 5 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013; and
Instruct the LDRRM Council to incorporate the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in its LDRRMF Investment Plan.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Strengthen the gender-responsive monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system by updating the composition of the GAD M&E Team;
Subsequently, conduct regular assessment of institutional mechanisms for gender mainstreaming to monitor the effectiveness and efficiency of GAD implementation; and
Prepare and ensure the timely submission of the annual LGU Status Report on Institutional Mechanisms for Gender Mainstreaming to the DILG Regional Office.

We recommended that management: 

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to file a request for authority to write-off for accounts deemed dormant and liquidation is futile, following the procedures contained in COA Circular 2023-08 dated August 17, 2023; and

In the case of those who have been separated from service, make representations with the proper prosecuting agencies of the government towards the institution of the necessary proceedings for the recovery of the amount of the cash advance as provided in Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 86-256.
observations.reason:
Employees who are granted cash advances do not have any outstanding unliquidated advances. Therefore, there is no need to withhold their salary.

Demand letters were sent to active National Government employees but so far, no payment has been received yet. Although amounts involved are minimal.

Not prioritized by the Municipal Accountant due to complexity of procedures and required documents.
Not implemented due to difficulty in implementing all the required procedures on one-time cleansing of PPE

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw adjusting entries to correct the misstated accounts.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were made to correct the misstated accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management: 

Direct the Municipal Accountant to present the correction of prior period adjustments/errors by restating the comparative amounts for the period(s) presented in which adjustments/errors occurred, whenever practicable; and

Provide adequate disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements to achieve fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
No prior period errors in CY 2024

The Municipal Accountant overlooked the preparation of disclosures for the Adjustment of Net Revenue Recognized Directly in Net Assets/Equity under the Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Equity, LDRRMF and Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit)
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant overlooked the preparation of disclosures for the Adjustment of Net Revenue Recognized Directly in Net Assets/Equity under the Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Equity, LDRRMF and Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit)

The Municipality availed of LBP Cash Pick-up System every Wednesday and Friday.

The Municipal Treasurer is still compiling the required RCDs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Require all persons responsible in the vehicles and equipment rental processes to impose the prescribed rental rates and strictly observe the control activities presented in Municipal Ordinance No. 259-2023; and

Incorporate the rental hours and rates in the pro-forma Heavy Equipment Lease Agreement.
observations.managementAction: Already imposed the prescribed rental rates and observed the control activities outlined in Municipal Ordinance No. 259-2023.

Some unexpended balances were already returned to source agencies.  For the other balances, management will return it after they have established the source agencies and the status of said projects.

The Municipal Accountant reclassified the said balances to its appropriate accounts.
observations.reason:
Not completely returned pending the identification of source agencies.



Accounting Unit cannot trace the source of funds nor status of such projects



Not prioritized by the Municipal Accountant due to complexity of procedures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the requirement of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 by establishing procedures, which will ensure that cash pertaining to the unexpended balance of LDRRMF is actually transferred to the Trust Fund at year-end.
observations.managementAction: Transfer of unexpended balance of LDRRMF to Trust Fund was made at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the Treasury Circular on granting cash advances and bonding of public officials and employees and likewise evaluate the amount of bond coverage of its accountable officers and increase the coverage of the fidelity bonds whenever necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Fidelity Bond of the Disbursing Officer has already increased.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management:

Direct the Municipal Assessor to prepare an updated
Assessment Roll based on the 2018 General Revision of Real Property Assessments preferably in electronic format and furnish the same to the Municipal Treasurer;

Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the duly certified list of taxpayers with corresponding amount of tax due and collectible for the year based on the updated Assessment Roll; and 

Henceforth, direct the Municipal Accountant to set-up RPT/SET Receivables based on the duly certified list of taxpayers with corresponding amount of tax due and collectible, for proper presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Assessor committed to fully implement 
the recommendation in CY 2024 in order for the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to update their respective records.
observations.reason:
Due to voluminous number of real property units, update 
of Assessment Roll was not completed as of year-end

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the Treasury Circular on granting cash advances and bonding of public officials and employees and likewise evaluate the amount of bond coverage of its accountable officers and increase the coverage of the fidelity bonds whenever necessary.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Increase in amount of fidelity bond premium was not included in the budget.

observations.recommendation: Direct the MDRRMO to incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the detailed planned projects/program/activities chargeable to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years and those funds under the Trust Liability – DRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to comply with the audit recommendation in CY 2024.
observations.reason: Not prioritized by the management.

observations.managementAction: Management committed to comply with all of the recommendations.
observations.reason: Not prioritized by the management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Enjoin the Municipal Assessor to fast track the preparation of the updated Assessment Roll and provide a copy to the Municipal Treasurer as basis for updating the RPTARs/RPTICs;
Thereafter direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Update the RPTARs/RPTICs and prepare a duly certified list of taxpayers with corresponding amount of tax due and collectible for the year which shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in the set-up of RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year; and
Prepare a complete list of Delinquent Taxpayers and post in appropriate public places as required  under Section 254 of R.A. 7160.
We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw adjusting entries to correct the misstated accounts.

We recommended that management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to present the correction of prior period adjustments/errors by restating the comparative amounts for the period(s) presented in which adjustments/errors occurred, whenever practicable; and
Provide adequate disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements to achieve fair presentation of the financial statements.

observations.managementAction: The LGU already required all departments to submit inventory of existing properties in their respective offices and consequently identify the serviceable and unserviceable items.    As recommended, the municipality will create an Inventory Committee to conduct the one-time cleansing of Property, Plant and Equipment.
observations.reason: Lack of enough manpower, absence of prior years’ records and difficulty in implementing all the required procedures on the one-time cleansing of PPE.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Instruct the municipal accountant to draw a correcting entry for transactions misclassified under the Accounts Payable account; and

Require strictly the submission of appropriate supporting documents as proof of receipt of goods and services before recognizing payables in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented
observations.reason: The recommendations were fully implemented by the management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to observe proper recording of transactions pertaining to fund transfers from the National Government following the pro-forma entries:




Instruct the concerned accounting staff to dig-up records to substantiate the adjustments/change in forwarded balance in the CY 2021 GL for CIP-Infrastructures Assets and Trust Liabilities under the TF-BUB Fund, otherwise reverse the entry made and effect the proposed adjusting entry as follows:




Require the accounting staff to maintain complete and appropriate Subsidiary Ledgers to support the accounts Trust Liabilities, Due to NGAs and Construction in Progress accounts; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to adequately review the work of the accounting staff to avoid recurrence of the same deficiency.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented
observations.reason: The recommendations were fully implemented by the management.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasure to:

Prepare a complete list of Real Property Tax delinquencies for all barangays.  If necessary, request for augmentation of personnel to regularly prepare and update the list; and

Enjoin the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the provision as mandated under Section 254 of the Local Government Code for the posting requirement of the Notice of Delinquency as a condition on availing judicial or administrative remedies which the Provincial Treasury Office may pursue.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented
observations.reason: The recommendations were fully implemented by the management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Follow strictly the guidelines as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2013-004 and its applicable annexes and templates in the installation of project signboards; 

Include the cost of signboards in the Approved Budget for the Contract and Program of Works; and

For projects implemented by contract, include the installation of signboards as a checklist item in the supporting documents before obligating payment for the first partial billing to the contractors.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented
observations.reason: The recommendations were fully implemented by the management.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the GAD Focal Point System, in coordination with the Local Planning and Development Office, (a) create a GAD database to include gender statistics and other relevant sex-disaggregated data, as basis in GAD planning, programming and policymaking; and (b) timely submit to the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Report.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Streamline and document its donation policy and process thru the issuance of an Executive Order or a Memorandum, which among others, would contain the employees authorized to receive donations and their reportorial responsibilities.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the PPE in accordance with the criteria set under Item 4.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to make necessary adjusting entries to effect the dropping of the PPE costing below ₱15,000.00 and provide a copy of the JEV to the GSO together with the schedule or list of affected PPE for their subsequent dropping from the property/equipment records.

We recommended that management:
Require the MNAO, MAO and RHU to submit to the MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant the Acknowledgement Receipts of the donations they have received and the list of beneficiaries with whom the donations were distributed, copy furnished the Office of the Auditor;
Streamline and document its donation policy and process thru the issuance of an Executive Order or a Memorandum, which among others, would contain the employees authorized to receive donations and their reportorial responsibilities.

Management comment:
The management concurred to act on the auditor’s recommendation.

We recommended the management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the PPE in accordance with the criteria set under Item 4.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to make necessary adjusting entries to effect the dropping of the PPE costing below ₱15,000.00 and provide a copy of the JEV to the GSO together with the schedule or list of affected PPE for their subsequent dropping from the property/equipment records.

Management comment:
The Municipal Accountant committed to reclassify and prepare journal entry voucher to effect the dropping of the PPE below the ₱15,000.00 threshold.

We recommended the management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to make necessary adjusting entries to record the transfer of property and equipment under the Trust Fund to General Fund. Corresponding depreciation expenses shall then be provided in the books in accordance with Section 4(o) of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I. Moreover, Municipal Accountant to comply with the prescribed accounting entries in the recording of financial transactions under the TF-LDRRMF as prescribed by COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

Management comment:
The Municipal Accountant committed to draw journal entry voucher to record the transfer of property and equipment from the trust fund to general fund books and corresponding depreciation expense will be provided.

We recommended the management to instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to closely coordinate with the Local Chief Executive and other members of the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) for the implementation of development projects under the LGU’s investment plan to ensure that these plans are carried out on a timely manner and its purpose be achieved to the benefit of its constituents.

Management comment:
The management stressed that most infrastructure projects and programs have been delayed due to other social and economic projects implemented during the pandemic particularly the Ligtas Covid-19 Facility and other related activities which transported and housed hundreds of returning OFWs, residents, and individuals, and implementation of relief programs and support for the disadvantaged groups and communities. Municipal Engineer explained that some infrastructure projects were delayed due to unfavourable weather conditions and unavailability of construction materials and scheduling of heavy equipment.

We recommended that management maximize the utilization of the 5% LDRRM fund to strengthen preparations for disaster risk reduction and management in compliance with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

Management comment:
The MDRRMO stressed that the program/projects/activities allocated under the QRF were not exhausted especially the allocation for the purchase of vaccines. According to the rural health unit, there was an adequate number of vaccines used and distributed during the vaccination roll-out activities. The Management concurred with the audit recommendation to maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF and will prioritize programs/projects/activities which resonate with the mitigation and reduction of potential disasters and calamities.

We recommended that management:
Directs MDRRMO to incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the detailed planned projects/program/activities chargeable to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years and those funds under the Trust Liability – DRRMF;
Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate registries and subsidiary records by year of transfer for the LDRRMF; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose separately the LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended/unobligated balance of LDRRMF.

Management comment:
The management concurred that the reprogramming of the TF-MDRRMF will be put into a detailed plan in the same manner as reflected in the LDRRMFIP. Also, they commit to maintain separate registries and subsidiary records by year of transfer for the LDRRMF and will disclose separately the amount and details of unexpended/unobligated balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the insurance premium covering the whole year be taken up as Prepaid Insurance and only the portion of the prepayment that has been used up be recognized as Insurance Expense. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw an adjusting entry for the reclassification.

We recommended that management:
Directs Municipal Accountant to maintain supplies ledger cards for each type of supplies and reconcile these with the stock cards maintained by the Treasurer’s Office or the General Services Office; and
Require also the GSO Designate/Municipal Treasurer to consolidate and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to the Municipal Accountant as the basis to record issuances and appropriate expenditure accounts to ensure a fair presentation of the balance per books at the end of the respective month; and
Require the concerned personnel in charge of inventory management to conduct periodic physical inventory count of supplies and submit Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively.

Management comment:
The Office of the Municipal Accountant concurred to the audit recommendations of maintaining supplies and reconcile these with the stock cards maintained by the Treasurer’s Office/General Service Office. The GSO also concurred on preparation and maintenance of stock cards, RIS and SSMI.

We recommended the municipal accountant to reclassify the current and non-current portion of the long-term loans payable to Loans Payable – Current, Domestic account and Loans Payable, Long-Term Domestic, respectively and present these payables under the appropriate category in the financial statements.

Management comment:
The management concurred to reclassify the current and non-current portion of the long-terms loans payable in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that management faithfully complies with the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 to effectively control fuel, gasoline, and oil expenditures to avoid wasteful, excessive, and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption by implementing the following corrective measures:
Exercise tighter control over the use of government vehicles through properly accomplished and duly approved serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets. Moreover, Request for Issue Voucher or equivalent should be prepared with sufficient information indicated.
Always ensure that submission of the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels are complete in form and content.

Management comment:
The management concurred to comply with the audit recommendations.

The Municipality should secure prior written conformity and acquiescence of the Solicitor General and the written concurrence of the COA, in the cases provided under in par. 3(1), Section 481 of R.A. 7160 and COA Circular 98-002 in the hiring of private lawyer, and;
Comply with the documentary requirements in hiring special counsel to appear in court in pursuance to COA Circular 2012-001.

We have recommended that Management:

Require the RHU In-Charge to submit inventory report upon acquisition of donated drugs and medicines to the Accounting Office; and

Require the RHU Inventory In-Charge to submit the monthly report of issuances to the Municipal Accountant as basis for the recording of expenses for inventories consumed or distributed; and

Direct the RHU Inventory In-Charge to prepare and update the stock cards regularly and reconcile it with the results of physical inventory count at least on a semestral basis.
observations.reason: The donated drugs and medicines from prior years were already recorded in the books. However, the RHU In-charge was still not able to submit inventory report upon acquisition of donated drugs and medicines and monthly reports of issuances to the Municipal Accountant thus, current year donated drugs and medicines were not recorded in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the MDRRMO thru coordination with concerned municipal officials to submit to the OCD and the COA a complete list of donations received and distributed by the municipality.
observations.reason: The MDRRMO was not able to comply with the reporting requirements for the Donations in Kind received by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management to organize a validation team who will work in close coordination with barangay officials concerned and the DSWD to conduct verification on the eligibility of the aforementioned beneficiaries based on the filled-up SAC; and

Direct the validation team to submit to the audit team a report/status of their verification and the actions taken by management and liable persons thereon.
observations.reason: The MSWDO have already submitted the results of their verification on the affected beneficiaries which were concluded as among the qualified beneficiaries for the Social Amelioration Program (SAP).

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the pertinent lacking supporting documents in order to establish the propriety of the affected transactions.
observations.reason: The lacking documents were already submitted by the Office of the Municipal Accountant to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to require the BAC to observe strict compliance on the mandatory posting of the procurement data for all contracts awarded under Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act pursuant to GPPB Circular No. 01-2020.
observations.reason: The BAC Secretariat was still unable to log in to the GPPB Online Portal.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management:

Require the Accounting Office to record first the purchases of drugs and medicines supplies as inventory and equipments to respective PPE classification; and

Require the RHU Inventory In-Charge to submit the monthly report of issuances to the Municipal Accountant as basis for the recording of expenses for inventoried consumed or distributed; and 
Direct the RHU Inventory In-Charge to prepare and update the stock cards regularly and reconcile it with the results of physical inventory count at least on a semestral basis; and 

Require the Municipal Accountant proper charging of accounts and reclass accounts affected.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant had already effected adjustments in the books of the LGU and current year procurements of drugs and medicines were already recorded as part of the Inventories account.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that Management:

Prioritize the completion of inventory taking and submit the RPCPPE; and 

Direct the Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to maintain updated Supplies/Property Ledger Cards and Stock/Property and Equipment Cards, respectively, using the prescribed format as provided under the NGAS for LGUs.  The assigned personnel should further be instructed to make a regular reconciliation of records and monitor the utilization of supplies; and

The physical inventory of supplies and property, plant and equipment shall be reconciled with the Supplies/Property and Equipment Ledger Cards as maintained in the Accounting Office and Stock/Property Cards as maintained in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, any discrepancies should be immediately verified and adjusted.
observations.reason:
The inventory-taking of the PPE accounts was not completed due to lack of manpower. The Municipal Accountant and GSO-designate were able to prepare the PPELCs and PCs, respectively. However, these PPE records were still incomplete, and regular reconciliation of records was not made. 

Reiterated on this year’s Audit Report with modification.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: 

Require the concerned LGU officers and inventory committee to render at the end of each accounting period all the necessary inventory reports and ledger and property cards following the required format prescribed in COA Circular 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015 (attached as Annexes A, B, D to E in the Circular); and

Require the Municipal Engineer to assess and evaluate the projects recorded in the Road Networks account and provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO (Local Treasurer, in the absence of GSO) a complete and detailed description and cost segregation of the road components indicating specific measurements of the surface type, maintenance cost, initial costs incurred or valuation, date of completion, location and other necessary data; and 

Require the Municipal Accountant to recognize depreciation on every depreciable road component once all the necessary details are identified; and

Require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO (Local Treasurer, in the absence of GSO) to reconcile its Road Network records in order to present an accurate, reliable, complete and accurate valuation of the Road Network account.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer commits to assess and evaluate the projects recorded in the Road Network Account and maintain Report on Local Road Network, Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) strictly adhere to Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and Section 13.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and (b) submit immediately the lacking documentary requirements.
observations.reason: The lacking documents were already submitted by the Office of the Municipal Accountant to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management:
Require the RHU In-Charge to submit inventory report upon acquisition of donated drugs and medicines to the Accounting Office; and
Require the RHU Inventory In-Charge to submit the monthly report of issuances to the Municipal Accountant as basis for the recording of expenses for inventories consumed or distributed; and
Direct the RHU Inventory In-Charge to prepare and update the stock cards regularly and reconcile it with the results of physical inventory count at least on a semestral basis.

We recommended that management instruct the MDRRMO thru coordination with concerned municipal officials to submit to the OCD and the COA a complete list of donations received and distributed by the municipality.

We recommended that management to organize a validation team who will work in close coordination with barangay officials concerned and the DSWD to conduct verification on the eligibility of the aforementioned beneficiaries based on the filled-up SAC; and
Direct the validation team to submit to the audit team a report/status of their verification and the actions taken by management and liable persons thereon.
We have recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the pertinent lacking supporting documents in order to establish the propriety of the affected transactions.

We recommended the management to require the BAC to observe strict compliance on the mandatory posting of the procurement data for all contracts awarded under Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act pursuant to GPPB Circular No. 01-2020.

We have recommended that Management:
Require the Accounting Office to record first the purchases of drugs and medicines supplies as inventory and equipments to respective PPE classification; and
Require the RHU Inventory In-Charge to submit the monthly report of issuances to the Municipal Accountant as basis for the recording of expenses for inventoried consumed or distributed; and
Direct the RHU Inventory In-Charge to prepare and update the stock cards regularly and reconcile it with the results of physical inventory count at least on a semestral basis; and
Require the Municipal Accountant proper charging of accounts and reclass accounts affected.

We reiterated our recommendations that Management:
Prioritize the completion of inventory taking and submit the RPCPPE; and
Direct the Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to maintain updated Supplies/Property Ledger Cards and Stock/Property and Equipment Cards, respectively, using the prescribed format as provided under the NGAS for LGUs.  The assigned personnel should further be instructed to make a regular reconciliation of records and monitor the utilization of supplies; and
The physical inventory of supplies and property, plant and equipment shall be reconciled with the Supplies/Property and Equipment Ledger Cards as maintained in the Accounting Office and Stock/Property Cards as maintained in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, any discrepancies should be immediately verified and adjusted.

We recommended that Management:
Require the concerned LGU officers and inventory committee to render at the end of each accounting period all the necessary inventory reports and ledger and property cards following the required format prescribed in COA Circular 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015 (attached as Annexes A, B, D to E in the Circular); and
Require the Municipal Engineer to assess and evaluate the projects recorded in the Road Networks account and provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO (Local Treasurer, in the absence of GSO) a complete and detailed description and cost segregation of the road components indicating specific measurements of the surface type, maintenance cost, initial costs incurred or valuation, date of completion, location and other necessary data; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to recognize depreciation on every depreciable road component once all the necessary details are identified; and
Require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO (Local Treasurer, in the absence of GSO) to reconcile its Road Network records in order to present an accurate, reliable, complete and accurate valuation of the Road Network account.

We recommended that Management (a) strictly adhere to Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and Section 13.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and (b) submit immediately the lacking documentary requirements.

We have recommended to management to direct the Municipal Accountant to give utmost priority in the preparation and submission of monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements as required or provided by COA Circular No. 96-011; and

Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue the conduct of the monthly reconciliation of the cash accounts balances to facilitate smooth preparation and timely submission of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements.
observations.managementAction: On-going

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the Management to Require the HRMO in-charge to act on and/or process a complete retirement application within the period provided, and
Strictly comply with the provisions of RA 10154 and its IRR and give the highest priority to the payment of other retirement benefits particularly the Terminal Benefits once all the requirements are complied by the retiring employee.

We have recommended to Management to barangay shares should be remitted quarterly to the LGUs or barangays concerned in accordance with the provisions of Section 271(d) of RA 7160; and

The Municipal Treasurer should be required to submit the report of collections to the Accountant within five (5) days of the following quarter when the receipts were made for the preparation of disbursement voucher.

We have recommended to management to observe the principle of the DILG in conducting future similar activities within the territorial jurisdiction of the municipality in adherence to the principle of prudent use of scarce financial resources of the government.  

Justify also in writing why the registration fee was paid to Mis. Occ. League of Local Planning and Dev. Coord., Inc.

We have recommended to management to strengthen their controls in the use of motor vehicles by complying with the provisions of COA Circular No, 77-61 and Section 361e. of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual such as issuance of pre-numbered driver’s trip tickets; 

Ensure proper markings of the vehicles with the name and/or logo of the Municipality with “For Official Use Only” should be made pursuant to Joint Circular No. 1 dated September 3, 1991 of the COA and DBM to prevent unauthorized use of the same;

We have recommended to management to Management should comply strictly with the provisions provided by the aforementioned laws and secure the required Environmental Compliance Certificate to avoid further penal and administrative liabilities; and
    
     The Management to effectively and efficiently observe the implementation of the 10-year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan.

observations.managementAction:
Only partial inventory reports per office were submitted to COA and not the required RPCPPE.  Also, not all inventory and PPE accounts were included in said report.

Hence, this finding is reiterated in this report as finding no. 7.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Management to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to give utmost priority in the preparation and submission of monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements as required or provided by COA Circular No. 96-011; and
Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue the conduct of the monthly reconciliation of the cash accounts balances to facilitate smooth preparation and timely submission of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements.

We have recommended to Management the following:
Require the HRMO in-charge to act on and/or process a complete retirement application within the period provided, and
Strictly comply with the provisions of RA 10154 and its IRR and give the highest priority to the payment of other retirement benefits particularly the Terminal Leave Benefits once all the requirements are complied with by the retiring employee.

We have recommended to the Management the following:
Barangay shares should be remitted quarterly to the LGUs or barangays concerned in accordance with the provisions of Section 271(d) of RA 7160; and
The Municipal Treasurer should be required to submit the report of collections to the Accountant within five (5) days of the following quarter when the receipts were made for the preparation of disbursement voucher.

We have recommended to management the following:
Observe the principle of the DILG in conducting future similar activities within the territorial jurisdiction of the municipality in adherence to the principle of prudent use of scarce financial resources of the government.
Justify also in writing why the registration fee was paid to Mis. Occ. League of Local Planning and Dev. Coord., Inc.

We have recommended the following:
1.	That Management should comply strictly with the provisions provided by the aforementioned laws and secure the required Environmental Compliance Certificate to avoid further penal and administrative liabilities; and
2.	The Management to effectively and efficiently observe the implementation of the 10-year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan.

We have reiterated the following audit recommendations to Management:
1.	To prioritize the completion of inventory taking and submit the RPCPPE;
2.	Augment personnel in the Accounting Office as well as in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to be assigned in updating the Supplies/ Property Ledger Cards and Stock/Property and Equipment Cards, respectively, using the prescribed format as provided under the NGAS for LGUs.  The assigned personnel should further be instructed to make a regular reconciliation per class type of PPE account or records and monitor the utilization of supplies; and
3.	The physical inventory of supplies and property, plant and equipment shall be reconciled with the Supplies/Property and Equipment Ledger Cards as maintained in the Accounting Office and Stock/Property Cards as maintained in the GSO/Municipal Treasurer’s Office, any discrepancies should be immediately verified and adjusted.

We have recommended to the Management to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to give utmost priority in the preparation and submission of monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements as required or provided by COA Circular No. 96-011; and
Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue the conduct of the monthly reconciliation of the cash accounts balances to facilitate smooth preparation and timely submission of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements.

We have recommended to Management the following:
Require the HRMO in-charge to act on and/or process a complete retirement application within the period provided, and
Strictly comply with the provisions of RA 10154 and its IRR and give the highest priority to the payment of other retirement benefits particularly the Terminal Leave Benefits once all the requirements are complied with by the retiring employee.

We have recommended to the Management the following:
Barangay shares should be remitted quarterly to the LGUs or barangays concerned in accordance with the provisions of Section 271(d) of RA 7160; and
The Municipal Treasurer should be required to submit the report of collections to the Accountant within five (5) days of the following quarter when the receipts were made for the preparation of disbursement voucher.

We have recommended to management the following:
Observe the principle of the DILG in conducting future similar activities within the territorial jurisdiction of the municipality in adherence to the principle of prudent use of scarce financial resources of the government.
Justify also in writing why the registration fee was paid to Mis. Occ. League of Local Planning and Dev. Coord., Inc.

We have recommended the following:
1.	That Management should comply strictly with the provisions provided by the aforementioned laws and secure the required Environmental Compliance Certificate to avoid further penal and administrative liabilities; and
2. 	The Management to effectively and efficiently observe the implementation of the 10-year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan.

We have reiterated the following audit recommendations to Management:
1.	To prioritize the completion of inventory taking and submit the RPCPPE;
2.	Augment personnel in the Accounting Office as well as in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to be assigned in updating the Supplies/ Property Ledger Cards and Stock/Property and Equipment Cards, respectively, using the prescribed format as provided under the NGAS for LGUs.  The assigned personnel should further be instructed to make a regular reconciliation per class type of PPE account or records and monitor the utilization of supplies; and
3. 	The physical inventory of supplies and property, plant and equipment shall be reconciled with the Supplies/Property and Equipment Ledger Cards as maintained in the Accounting Office and Stock/Property Cards as maintained in the GSO/Municipal Treasurer’s Office, any discrepancies should be immediately verified and adjusted.

We have recommended to management to require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit/deposit intact all collections in their possession daily or the next banking day pursuant to the above-mentioned criteria; and

Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to monitor daily the collections and deposits so as to avoid the accumulation of Cash in Vault and to maximize potential benefits it would bring to the agency.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to closely monitorthe compliance and implementation of the provisions under RA 9003.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Management to submit liquidation upon utilization.  If no liquidation is submitted by the recipient of funds within the required time for submission, the municipality should not grant additional fund transfer to the same recipient; and

Follow strictly the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: Out of 7, only five (5) barangays have already submitted their liquidations.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to Management that the Municipal Accountant should closely monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ensure its immediate liquidation and return of excess funds thereof so that government funds are fully maximized for the delivery of basic services that promotesgood governance and to prevent possible loss or misappropriation.
observations.reason: It has been practiced that cash advances are liquidated the following month, at a minimum amount.  Most of the unliquidated as of December 31 were liquidated in January or even after.  The cash advance balances were mostly those who were no longer connected in the LGU and some were already deceased.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to Management to require the concerned officers/personnel to submit all the required reports within the prescribed deadline and monitor compliance with the timely submission of reports; and

observations.reason:

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to Management the following:
a) 	To require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit/deposit intact all collections in their possession daily or the next banking day pursuant to the above-mentioned criteria; and
b) 	To require the Municipal Treasurer to monitor daily the collections and deposits so as to avoid the accumulation of Cash in Vault and to maximize potential benefits it would bring to the agency.

We have recommended to Management the following:
a) 	Submit liquidation upon utilization.  If no liquidation is submitted by the recipient of funds within the required time for submission, the municipality should not grant additional fund transfer to the same recipient; and
b) 	Follow strictly the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We have recommended to Management that the Municipal Accountant should closely monitor the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ensure its immediate liquidation and return of excess funds thereof so that government funds are fully maximized for the delivery of basic services that promotes good governance and to prevent possible loss or misappropriation.

We have recommended to Management that:
Technical working group and GAD focal person should closely coordinate with the heads of office on the implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities based on approved plans and budgets;
Strictly comply with the submission of the Accomplishment Report as mandated by COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014; and
Implement programs, projects and activities only for which the GAD funds have been appropriated.

We have recommended to Management the following:
a) 	Require the concerned officers/personnel to submit all the required reports within the prescribed deadline and monitor compliance with the timely submission of reports; and
b) 	Cause the suspension of payment of salaries of the concerned officials until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission in accordance with Section 122 of PD 1445.

We reiterated the following audit recommendations to Management:
1. 	To prioritize the completion of inventory taking and submit the RPCPPE;
2. 	To augment personnel in the Accounting Office as well as in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to be assigned in maintaining an updated Supplies/Property Ledger Cards and Stock/Property and Equipment Cards, respectively, using the prescribed format as provided under the NGAS for LGUs.  The assigned personnel should further be instructed to make a regular reconciliation of records and monitor the utilization of supplies; and
3. To reconcile the physical inventory of supplies and property, plant and equipment with the Supplies/Property and Equipment Ledger Cards as maintained in the Accounting Office and Stock/Property Cards as maintained in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, any discrepancies should be immediately verified and adjusted.

We have recommended to management to require the Municipal Accountant to make a Certification that the previous cash advance/s are first settled and accounted for in the books before granting additional cash advance to the concerned officials and employees; and

Moreover, require all accountable officers to settle their cash advances at year-end and to strictly monitor periodically the submission of liquidation reports to ensure timely accounting thereof to avoid undue accumulation of outstanding cash advances and return of excess cash advance thereof so that government funds are fully maximized for the delivery of basic services that promotes good governance and to forestall possible loss through theft or misappropriation.

We have recommended to management to secure adequate fidelity bond equivalent to the maximum accountability of accountable officer in order to safeguard the assets in case of loss  through theft, misappropria-tion and other loss due to fortuitous events; to monitor properly the change in accountability of each accountable officer and see to it that the amount of their bond premium conforms to their amount of bond; and to appropriate yearly a budget to cover for the payment of premiums on the renewal of the fidelity bonds both for public funds and public property.
observations.reason: For full implementation upon renewal of the Fidelity Bond

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the following audit recommendations to management to prioritize the completion of inventory taking and submit the RPCPPE; augment personnel in the Accounting Office as well as in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to be assigned in maintaining an updated Supplies/Property Ledger Cards and Stock/Property and Equipment Cards, respectively, using the prescribed format as provided under the NGAS for LGUs. The assigned personnel should further be instructed to make a regular reconciliation of records and monitor the utilization of supplies; and the physical inventory of supplies and property, plant and equipment shall be reconciled with the Supplies/Property and Equipment Ledger Cards as maintained in the Accounting Office and Stock /Property Cards as maintained in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, any discrepancies should be immediately verified and adjusted.
observations.reason: Maintained Property Stock Card but reconciliation as to balances of PPE Accounts has not been made, thus reiterated.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management should direct the Property Custodian or Municipal Treasurer and its General Services Office Staff to accomplish the IIRUP and to take immediate steps to dispose all unserviceable properties of the municipality to prevent possible loss and avoid further deterioration, wear and tear and reduction in values, in accordance with the guidelines provided by Sections 167, 169 and 170 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated Oct. 20, 1992.
observations.reason: RPCPPE for the municipality’s properties was not completely submitted yet, thus, Inventory Report of Unserviceable Properties could not also be prepared or determined.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Management to instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the OIC-JMEEDO Manager to segregate in their cashbooks each special or economic enterprise account income and operating costs for easy determination of net income; and to instruct the Municipal Accountant to segregate each special account according to its distinctiveness and reflect the actual operations in each of the special accounts affected pursuant to Section 106 of COA Circular No. 2002-003.
We have recommended to Management that the unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE be transferred to the Special Trust Fund using the following entries;

We have recommended to Management that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should strictly observe the provisions of R.A. 9184 particularly the posting of procurement results in the Government Electronic Procurement System (G-EPS) bulletin board, the provision of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and DILG Memo Circular No. 2010-83.
observations.reason: The BAC Secretary started to post the NOA and NTP.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to Management that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should ensure compliance to the requirements of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of R.A. 9184.
observations.reason: Prepared the Procurement Monitoring Report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Management to require the IBAMO, Inc. to submit the lacking documentary require-ments prescribed under COA Circular No. 2007-001.
observations.reason: The management communicated with the secretary of IBAMO, Inc. for submission of lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to management should instruct the General Services Officer to      furnish     copies      of Acceptance and Inspection Reports, together with the Delivery Receipts to the Audit Team within twenty-four hours from acceptance to enable them to conduct timely validation thereof pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer/ General Services Office Staff explained that this submission within 24 hours was not done before for the reason that the signatories of the Copies of Notices of Deliveries/Inspection & Acceptance Reports, most often were not around during the deliveries.

observations.recommendation: The Management should direct the Property Officer to dispose all unserviceable properties of the LGU in accordance with Section 167, 169 and 170 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated Oct. 20, 1992.
observations.managementAction:
Reiterated with some modifications as Finding No. 4 of this report.

Hence, RPCPPE for the municipality’s properties was not completely submitted yet, thus, Inventory Report of Unserviceable Properties could not also be prepared or determined.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to Management the following:
1.	To require the Municipal Accountant to make a Certification that the previous cash advance/s are first settled and accounted for in the books before granting additional cash advance to the concerned officials and employees; and
2.	Moreover, require all accountable officers to settle their cash advances at year-end and to strictly monitor periodically the submission of liquidation reports to ensure timely accounting thereof to avoid undue accumulation of outstanding cash advances and return of excess cash advance thereof so that government funds are fully maximized for the delivery of basic services that promotes good governance and to forestall possible loss through theft or misappropriation.

We have recommended to Management the following:
1.	To secure adequate fidelity bond equivalent to the maximum accountability of accountable officer in order to safeguard the assets in case of loss through theft, misappropriation and other loss due to fortuitous events;
2.	To monitor properly the change in accountability of each accountable officer and see to it that the amount of their bond premium conforms to their amount of bond; and
3.	To appropriate yearly a budget to cover for the payment of premiums on the renewal of the fidelity bonds both for public funds and public property.

We reiterated the following audit recommendations to Management:
1.	To prioritize the completion of inventory taking and submit the RPCPPE;
2.	Augment personnel in the Accounting Office as well as in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to be assigned in maintaining an updated Supplies/ Property Ledger Cards and Stock/Property and Equipment Cards, respectively, using the prescribed format as provided under the NGAS for LGUs.  The assigned personnel should further be instructed to make a regular reconciliation of records and monitor the utilization of supplies; and
3.	The physical inventory of supplies and property, plant and equipment shall be reconciled with the Supplies/Property and Equipment Ledger Cards as maintained in the Accounting Office and Stock/Property Cards as maintained in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, any discrepancies should be immediately verified and adjusted.

We have recommended that Management should direct the Property Custodian or Municipal Treasurer and its General Services Office Staff to accomplish the IIRUP and to take immediate steps to dispose all unserviceable properties of the municipality to prevent possible loss and avoid further deterioration, wear and tear and reduction in values, in accordance with the guidelines provided by Sections 167, 169 and 170 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.

We have recommended to Management the following:
1.	To instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the OIC-JMEEDO Manager to segregate in their cashbooks each special or economic enterprise account income and operating costs for easy determination of net income; and
2.	To instruct the Municipal Accountant to segregate each special account according to its distinctiveness and reflect the actual operations in each of the special accounts affected pursuant to Section 106 of COA Circular No. 2002-003.

We have recommended to Management that –
The unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE be transferred to the Special Trust Fund using the following entries:
We have recommended to Management that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should ensure compliance to the requirements of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of R.A. 9184.
We have recommended the Management to require the IBAMO, Inc. to submit the lacking documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2007-001.

We have recommended to management to conduct an inventory on all Property, Plant and Equipment and submit the reports to the Auditor as required in the regulation; and Correspondingly, Subsidiary Ledgers and Property Cards shall be prepared for the PPEs by the Accounting Office and General Services Office, respectively.
observations.managementAction:
Reiterated with some modifications as Finding No. 1 of this report.

The Local Government of Jimenez has created its Inventory Committee to facilitate timely actual physical inventory every year.
  
As of year-end, the physical count of all its properties though not yet completed, a physical count of inventory of its construction and heavy equipment and motor vehicles or transportation equipment was conducted and a partial Report of Physical Count on Heavy Equipment only of each office was prepared and submitted to the Office of the Auditor for compliance, verification and evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to management to require the Accounting Department to prepare separate trial balances for the special accounts in the General Fund most importantly for development projects funded out of the IRA that will facilitate reporting of funds utilized and review thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality’s Accounting Office already prepared this year 2017 separate trial balance for the 20% Development projects funded out of the IRA. While the special ac-counts for the economic enterprises in the GF this year 2018 only the agency will prepare or comply.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to management to require the Municipal Accounting Office should record supplies and materials purchased initially in the Inventory Account. Upon issuance thereof, the Inventory account shall be credited while the expense account shall be debited. The Accounting Office, the GSO or Municipal Treasurer are further advised to ensure compliance to the accounting procedures prescribed under Sections Nos. 51 and 114 of the NGAS-LGU.
observations.managementAction: To some procurement only the agency’s accounting office recorded it as Inventory account upon purchase for the reason that they don’t have enough stockroom or space to accommodate all their procured materials and inventories/supplies. Thus, recorded as expense once distributed to each office concerned though it is not completely consumed/ used.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to management to require GAD Focal Person to adhere to the PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01 and COA Circular 2014-001 in the preparation of GAD plans and budgets and GAD accomplishment reports so that there will be proper implementation, monitoring and performance evaluation of GAD related projects/ programs and activities.

observations.managementAction:
To strictly conduct an actual physical inventory-taking of all PPE acquired at least once a year through the Inventory Committee.

To strictly maintain and update Subsidiary Ledger and Property Card for each item of PPE including real properties.

To strictly prepare Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment in accordance with the NGAS Manual.

To properly place Property Identification
observations.reason:
Reiterated as Finding No. 1 of this report.

The Property Custodian, Mrs. Helen A. Cali-at, partially submitted her report on physical inventory on February 17, 2017 to the Office of the Auditor.

This Local Government Unit has created Inventory Committee to facilitate timely actual physical inventory every year.

The management should direct the Property Officer to dispose all unserviceable properties of the Local Government Unit in accordance with Section 167, 
169 and 170 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20,1992.

observations.managementAction:
Require the Accountable Officer to prepare monthly a Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms and submit the same to the Municipal Treasurer.

Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the consolidated Inventory and Statement of Accountability for Accountable Forms and submit it to the Unit Auditor not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month            as required by COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.reason:
Reports for the month of January were already submitted to the auditor on February 17, 2017.





No reports  was submitted by the Municipal Treasurer on Inventory and Statement of Accountabi-lity for Accountable Forms, Plates and Stickers.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to management to conduct an inventory on all Property, Plant and Equipment and submit the reports to the Auditor as required in the regulation; and Correspondingly, Subsidiary Ledgers and Property Cards shall be prepared for the PPEs by the Accounting Office and General Services Office, respectively.

We have recommended to management to require the Accounting Department to prepare separate trial balances for the special accounts in the General Fund most importantly for development projects funded out of the IRA that will facilitate reporting of funds utilized and review thereof.

We have recommended to management to require the Municipal Accounting Office should record supplies and materials purchased initially in the Inventory Account.  Upon issuance thereof, the Inventory account shall be credited while the expense account shall be debited.  The Accounting Office, the GSO or Municipal Treasurer are further advised to ensure compliance to the accounting procedures prescribed under Sections Nos. 51 and 114 of the NGAS-LGU.

We have recommended to management should instruct the General Services Officer to furnish copies of Acceptance and Inspection Reports, together with the Delivery Receipts to the Audit Team within twenty-four hours from acceptance to enable them to conduct timely validation thereof pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009.

We have recommended to management to require GAD Focal Person to adhere to the PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01 and COA Circular 2014-001 in the preparation of GAD plans and budgets and GAD accomplishment reports so that there will be proper implementation, monitoring and performance evaluation of GAD related projects/programs and activities.

observations.managementAction: The management should inquire with the National Treasury on the procedure for the return of the unexpended balance of PDAF and to ultimately return the amount to the unappropriated surplus in t general fund of the national government in compliance with the Supreme Court Decision dated November 19, 2013.

The LGU should specifically assigned   somebody to man the preparation and monitoring of the said reports.

The person assigned should then verify monthly the official  travels
and make sure that all trip tickets are properly and fully filled up.

Prepare and submit the reports to the Auditor as required in the regulation to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the month.
observations.reason:
The personnel assigned in the preparation of reports submitted on the last quarter of the year but lacks supporting documents.

Started January of this year, 2017, the Local Government Unit will prepare and submit monthly report of Official Travel and Fuel Consumption with attach trip tickets to the Accounting Office and to the office of the Auditor.

observations.managementAction: The procuring office should comply with the regulations and furnish the Unit Auditor with the required copies  of contract documents/pur-chase orders and their supporting documents as well as notice of deliveries of procured items within the prescribed period for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same before distribution to the end-users.
observations.reason:
Submission of contracts 
were  still oftentimes delayed, however, no purchase orders were submitted to the Office of the Auditor for review, evaluation and inspection of deliveries.
